docs.title: CityPortraits
docs.text: CityPortraits is a framework for reconstructing and analyzing urban systems through spatial data, historical sources, and computational models.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Develop a reproducible method to describe and compare cities through their building stock, spatial structure, and historical evolution.

docs.title: Framework
docs.text: ◻◻◻  Methodology Integrated analytical framework combining spatial, documentary, and data-driven processes. ○○○ Model Structured synthesis layer translating inputs into graphs, patterns, and urban representations. △△△ Case Studies Application of the framework to selected cities for comparison and validation.

docs.title: Perspective
docs.text: Further development of the framework will focus on extending city coverage, refining classification systems, strengthening cross-source reconciliation, and improving reproducible generation of urban scenarios.

docs.title: Index
docs.text: CityPortraits is a framework for reconstructing and analyzing urban systems through spatial data, historical sources, and computational models.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Develop a reproducible method to describe and compare cities through their building stock, spatial structure, and historical evolution.

docs.title: Framework
docs.text: ◻◻◻  Methodology Integrated analytical framework combining spatial, documentary, and data-driven processes. ○○○ Model Structured synthesis layer translating inputs into graphs, patterns, and urban representations. △△△ Case Studies Application of the framework to selected cities for comparison and validation.

docs.title: Perspective
docs.text: Further development of the framework will focus on extending city coverage, refining classification systems, strengthening cross-source reconciliation, and improving reproducible generation of urban scenarios.

docs.title: Methodology → IndexFramework for constructing comparable city portraits through spatial, documentary, and data-based analysis.
docs.text: The methodology translates heterogeneous sources into structured representations of urban systems, enabling reconstruction, comparison, and interpretation across contexts.

docs.title: Insights
docs.text:
Contextual Analysis

Extraction of historical, institutional, and narrative information from archives, publications, and actor networks. Outputs are structured descriptions of periods, decisions, and transformations.

Spatial Analysis

Derivation of urban structure from mapped data. Includes morphology, parcel logic, building distribution, and relationships between elements across scales.

Data Analysis

Quantification of attributes such as building types, densities, and distributions. Combines multiple datasets and resolves inconsistencies through alignment and calibration.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
01 Collection

Acquisition of spatial data, written sources, and statistical datasets.

02 Alignment

Linking sources through shared categories such as time periods, typologies, and locations.

03 Derivation

Extraction of patterns such as typological distributions, spatial structures, and temporal shifts.

04 Synthesis

Construction of consistent representations of the city for comparison across regions and time.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
Integration
  • Combine spatial, textual, and statistical sources within a shared structure
  • Translate qualitative descriptions into comparable attributes
  • Maintain links between scales such as building, block, city, and territory
Validation
  • Check consistency across datasets describing the same phenomena
  • Compare derived distributions with known references
  • Use historical and spatial constraints to limit implausible results
Traceability
  • Record source, assumptions, and transformation steps for each value
  • Allow multiple interpretations where necessary
  • Keep intermediate representations accessible

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text:
Urban System Reconstruction

Reconstruct building stock composition, spatial structure, and temporal layers from incomplete and heterogeneous sources.

Comparability

Enable comparison between cities by expressing them through a shared set of attributes and distributions.

Interpretation

Provide a basis for explaining how historical processes, planning decisions, and material constraints shape urban form.

docs.title: Methodology → Contextual Analysis → Index
docs.text: Written documentation supports spatial analysis by interpreting historical, cultural, and narrative sources.

docs.title: Historical Archive
docs.text: Official records, maps, and archival documents. → Historical Archive Media sources reflecting events, discourse, and urban change. → Newspaper Archives

docs.title: Narrative Studies
docs.text: Descriptive accounts and qualitative interpretations of urban life. → Narrative Studies Reports, studies, and institutional publications. → Documentary Research

docs.title: Actor
docs.text: → Actor - Architects - Urban Planners - Landscape Architects - Civil Engineers

docs.title: Approach
docs.text:
• Archival research
  • Media analysis
  • Literature review
  • Narrative interpretation

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: Provide context, support spatial findings, and capture the lived and historical dimensions of the city.

docs.title: Architect
docs.text:
A-001 IMAGE Gani Strazimiri c. 1950s–1980s — role: architect, planner, institutional figure Gani Strazimiri during the period of socialist urban planning in Albania
  • Key figure in shaping socialist-era urban planning institutions
  • Contributed to standardized housing and urban development models
  • Worked within central design institutes influencing national projects
Source: AQTN, Tirana

docs.title: Urban Planner
docs.text:
U-001 PLAN Koço Miho c. 1950s–1970s — type: planner / urbanist Representative socialist-era urban planning scheme (Tirana)
  • Contributed to centralized urban planning framework in post-war Albania
  • Involved in expansion and structuring of Tirana under socialist model
  • Worked within state institutes shaping regulatory and master plans
Source: AQTN, Tirana

docs.title: Landscape Architect
docs.text:
L-001 LANDSCAPE Thoma Thomai c. 1960s–1980s — type: horticulturist / landscape-related Development of public greenery and planted systems in Tirana
  • Contributed to development of urban green systems and parks
  • Worked on integration of vegetation into urban planning frameworks
  • Influenced agricultural and territorial planting strategies
Source: Agricultural and planning archives, Tirana

docs.title: Civil Engineer
docs.text:
E-001 INFRASTRUCTURE Ferdinand Toptani c. 1960s–1980s — type: civil engineer Engineering works contributing to national infrastructure systems
  • Worked on infrastructure and structural engineering systems
  • Contributed to development of transport and construction networks
  • Part of institutional engineering framework in socialist Albania
Source: AQTN, Tirana

docs.title: Methodology → Data Analysis → Index
docs.text: Data analysis structures and quantifies urban information to support spatial interpretation and modeling.

docs.title: Datasets
docs.text: Sources used to describe and model the urban system. → Datasets · GDE · GHSL · ASIG · OSM · Census

docs.text: Adjustment of data and models to align with known conditions. → Calibration

docs.title: Validation
docs.text: Evaluation of results against reference data and observations. → Validation

docs.title: Approach
docs.text:
• Data integration
  • Statistical analysis
  • Model calibration
  • Cross-source validation

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: Ensure consistency, reliability, and comparability of data-driven urban insights.

docs.title: Methodology → Data Analysis → Datasets → Index
docs.text: This section documents the primary data sources used for urban analysis and modeling in the CityPortraits project.

docs.title: Spatial Data
docs.text:
• GHSL - Global Human Settlement Layer: Built-up area mapping and population density
  • OSM - OpenStreetMap: Street networks, points of interest, and land use classification

docs.title: Administrative Data
docs.text:
• ASIG - Albanian Spatial Information Gateway: Official spatial data and administrative boundaries
  • GDE - Geographic Data Environment: Albanian national geospatial data platform

docs.title: Socio-Economic Data
docs.text: • Census - Population and housing statistics from national census data

docs.title: Methodology → Data Analysis → Datasets → Asig
docs.text: Albanian Spatial Information Gateway provides centralized access to spatial data and geographic information systems for Albania.

docs.title: Data Content
docs.text: Category Content Examples Base Maps Topographic and reference mapping 1:25,000 topographic maps, orthophotos, satellite imagery Geodetic Network Coordinate systems and reference points GNSS stations, geodetic control points, coordinate transformations Environmental Monitoring Natural resource and environmental data Protected areas, biodiversity zones, environmental monitoring sites Socio-Economic Data Demographic and economic spatial data Population distribution, economic indicators, social infrastructure Infrastructure Assets Public infrastructure and utilities Power lines, water networks, telecommunication infrastructure

docs.title: Applications in CityPortraits
docs.text: Application Area Use Cases Base Mapping Reference layer creation, coordinate system standardization, spatial reference establishment Environmental Assessment Biodiversity mapping, protected area analysis, environmental impact evaluation Infrastructure Analysis Utility network mapping, infrastructure asset management, service coverage analysis Socio-Economic Mapping Demographic analysis, economic development mapping, social service accessibility Geospatial Integration Data harmonization, coordinate system alignment, multi-source data integration

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: • Albanian Spatial Information Gateway

docs.title: Methodology → Data Analysis → Datasets → Census
docs.text: National census data provides demographic, housing, and socio-economic statistics for population analysis and urban planning.

docs.title: Data Content
docs.text: Category Content Examples Population Statistics Demographic counts and distributions Age groups, gender ratios, population density by administrative unit Housing Data Residential building and occupancy information Housing types, occupancy rates, building conditions, household sizes Economic Indicators Employment and income data Labor force participation, occupation types, income levels, economic activity Education Levels Educational attainment and literacy School attendance, educational qualifications, literacy rates Migration Patterns Population movement and mobility Internal migration, urban-rural flows, international migration Household Characteristics Family structure and living conditions Family size, household composition, access to services

docs.title: Applications in CityPortraits
docs.text: Application Area Use Cases Demographic Analysis Population distribution mapping, age structure analysis, gender balance assessment Housing Market Analysis Housing density mapping, residential development patterns, housing quality assessment Socio-Economic Planning Economic activity mapping, employment center identification, income distribution analysis Service Planning Education facility planning, healthcare access analysis, infrastructure demand assessment Urban Growth Modeling Population growth projections, migration impact analysis, settlement pattern prediction Equity Analysis Social service accessibility, economic disparity mapping, community needs assessment

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: • National Institute of Statistics Albania

docs.title: Methodology → Data Analysis → Datasets → Gde
docs.text: Global Dynamic Exposure project provides building-by-building exposure models for risk monitoring in natural catastrophes.

docs.title: Data Content
docs.text: Category Content Examples Building Footprints Individual building geometries Residential, commercial, and public buildings Building Properties Structural and occupancy attributes Building type, height, construction material, occupancy class Land Use Data CORINE land cover and urban classification Urban fabric, agricultural land, forest areas Administrative Divisions Qark, municipality, and commune boundaries Tirana municipality, Berat district, administrative units Infrastructure Networks Transportation and utility systems Road networks, power lines, water systems Geographic Features Natural and man-made features Rivers, elevation data, protected areas

docs.title: Exposure Indicators
docs.text: Indicator Type Data Sources Applications Building Exposure Footprints, properties, occupancy Earthquake risk assessment, flood vulnerability Vulnerability Assessment Construction type, age, resilience measures Structural vulnerability mapping, retrofit prioritization Urban Structure Land use, density, connectivity City morphology analysis, development pattern monitoring Dynamic Monitoring Temporal changes, urban growth Risk evolution tracking, climate adaptation planning

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text:
• GDE Data Explorer
  • Global Dynamic Exposure

docs.title: Integration
docs.text: GDE data provides the administrative framework for integrating OSM street data and GHSL settlement information.

docs.title: Methodology → Data Analysis → Datasets → Ghsl
docs.text: Global Human Settlement Layer provides global built-up area mapping and population density data for urban analysis.

docs.title: Data Content
docs.text: Category Content Examples Built-up Areas Urban and rural settlement mapping City centers, suburban areas, rural settlements Population Density Gridded population estimates High-density urban cores, medium-density suburbs Built-up Volume 3D building height and volume data High-rise districts, low-rise residential areas Settlement Classification Urban/rural typology Large urban areas, small towns, rural settlements Temporal Evolution Historical settlement changes Urban expansion patterns, growth trajectories

docs.title: Applications in CityPortraits
docs.text: Application Area Use Cases Urban Extent Mapping City boundary delineation, urban growth monitoring, sprawl analysis Population Distribution Demographic density mapping, urban population estimation, settlement patterns Built Environment Analysis Building density assessment, urban morphology studies, vertical development analysis Change Detection Historical urban transformation, development trend analysis, land use change monitoring

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text:
• GHSL Data Portal
  • European Commission JRC
  • Global Human Settlement Layer

docs.title: Integration
docs.text: GHSL data is combined with OSM for street network analysis and census data for demographic validation.

docs.title: Methodology → Data Analysis → Datasets → Osm
docs.text: OpenStreetMap provides street networks, points of interest, and land use data for Albanian cities.

docs.title: Data Content
docs.text: Category Content Examples Street Networks Road infrastructure and transportation Highways, streets, bridges, pedestrian paths Points of Interest Public and commercial facilities Schools, hospitals, shops, government buildings Land Use Urban zoning and development types Residential areas, commercial zones, industrial sites Green Spaces Parks and recreational areas City parks, gardens, sports facilities Transportation Public transit and mobility Bus stops, railway stations, parking areas

docs.title: Applications in CityPortraits
docs.text: Application Area Use Cases Urban Morphology Street pattern analysis, urban grain identification, block structure assessment Accessibility Analysis Service coverage mapping, transportation network evaluation, pedestrian accessibility Economic Indicators Commercial density mapping, business concentration analysis, economic activity assessment Land Use Planning Mixed-use development evaluation, zoning analysis, urban growth monitoring

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text:
• OpenStreetMap Albania
  • Geofabrik Albania Extracts
  • OSMnx Library

docs.text: OSM data is combined with GHSL for built-up area analysis and census data for demographic correlations.

docs.text: Spatial analysis examines the city as a layered system shaped by history, planning, and architecture.

docs.text: Block structure, street networks, and spatial patterns. → Urban Fabric · Morphology · Emergent Patterns

docs.text: Building-level analysis of typology, construction, and classification. → Architecture · Typology · Construction · Taxonomy

docs.text:
• Geospatial data (GIS, cadastral)
  • Historical sources
  • Computational methods
  • Architectural analysis

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: Reveal urban structure, link past and present, and support comparative city analysis.

docs.title: Graphs
docs.text:
Relational representation of spatial elements and their connections. → Graphs

Reset view Click a node to focus its neighborhood. Click the background or Reset view to return.

This is a sample network graph showing relationships between actors, events, and places. The actual graph will be rendered using the data from events.json, actors.json, and places.json.

docs.title: Patterns
docs.text:
Identification and abstraction of recurring spatial and architectural configurations. → Patterns

Morphological Analysis of urban form, street patterns, building density, and spatial structure. Architectural Study of building typologies, construction styles, and architectural evolution. Functional Examination of land use distribution, activity centers, and urban functionality.

docs.title: Approach
docs.text:
• Graph-based modeling
  • Pattern extraction
  • Rule-based systems
  • Computational representation

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: Translate urban complexity into structured models for analysis, comparison, and simulation.

docs.title: Tirana City Portrait
docs.text:
Area / Neighborhood Period of Evolution Streets / Axes Building Complexes Socio-Political Events Actors Historic Ottoman Tirana core pre-1920 → early 20th c.
  • Pazari i Ri
  • Et'hem Bey Mosque surroundings
  • Rruga Barrikadave
  • Courtyard houses
  • Bazaar fabric
  • Small plot mixed-use
  • Commercial strips
  • Ottoman urban structure
  • Tirana becomes capital (1920)
— Monarchy-era planned capital 1920–1940
  • Skanderbeg Square
  • Bulevardi Dëshmorët e Kombit
  • Rruga e Durrësit
  • Institutional ensembles
  • Perimeter blocks (neoclassical / rationalist)
  • Monumental civic complexes
  • Capital consolidation
  • Italian planning influence
  • Armando Brasini
  • Florestano Di Fausto
  • Gherardo Bosio
Socialist centre zone 1945–1990
  • Skanderbeg Square
  • Bulevardi Stalin / Dëshmorët e Kombit
  • Lana axis
  • Palace of Culture
  • National Historical Museum
  • Central institutions
  • State service buildings
  • Socialist reconstruction
  • Centralized planning
  • Ideological monumentalization
— Socialist housing estates 1950s–1980s
  • Rruga e Kavajës
  • Rruga e Dibrës
  • Rruga Myslym Shyri
  • Prefabricated apartment blocks
  • Standardized slab housing
  • Neighborhood units
  • State housing provision
  • Industrialized construction
— Shallvaret late socialist → post-socialist transition —
  • Mid-rise apartment blocks
  • Infill development
  • Late socialist housing intensification
  • Post-1990 transformation
— Kombinati socialist industrialization → post-1990
  • Kombinati axis
  • Worker housing
  • Industrial-residential ensembles
  • Industrial expansion
  • Post-socialist decline / transformation
— Urban edges / peripheral expansion 1990s → present —
  • Informal settlements
  • Self-built housing
  • Recent mixed-density expansion
  • Mass rural-urban migration
  • Informal urban growth
  • Regularization processes
— Archival Category Possible OSM Tag 0 Courtyard houses building=residential 1 Bazaar fabric landuse=commercial 2 Commercial strips shop=* 3 Institutional ensembles building=government 4 Monumental civic complexes building=public 5 Prefabricated apartment blocks building=apartments 6 Worker housing building=residential 7 Informal settlements building=residential 8 Industrial-residential ensembles landuse=industrial Make this Notebook Trusted to load map: File -> Trust Notebook

docs.title: Tirana City Portrait¶
docs.text:
Ongoing work

This notebook assembles a compact website-ready preview of the Tirana case study. It combines a small archival synthesis table, an OSM-oriented interpretation layer, and a schematic map preview.

docs.title: 1. Base Map Layers¶
docs.text:
The base map stack used in the broader analysis includes:

  • OpenStreetMap
  • Overture
  • GDE

docs.title: 2. Statistical Layers¶
docs.text:
Selected statistical layers used in the broader workflow:

  • Global Exposure Model
  • GHSL: Shape to Point
  • Census

docs.title: 3. Archival Data¶
docs.text: The table below summarizes selected areas and neighborhood types in Tirana discussed in archival and morphological literature. Streets are listed only where relatively precise references are available.

docs.title: 4. OSM-oriented interpretation¶
docs.text: This is not a strict data extraction step. It is a lightweight translation layer that connects archival building categories to possible OSM-facing tags or lookup ideas.

docs.title: 5. Map preview¶
docs.text: The map below is a schematic preview. It is meant for website communication rather than precise boundary mapping: selected streets are represented as curated editorial itineraries, and circles act as simple proxies for the relative prominence of selected urban areas.

docs.title: 6. Clustering¶
docs.text:
• Rule-based / event-based suggestions
  • Algorithm: spatial interpolation

docs.title: 7. Building Stock Scenarios¶
docs.text:
• Algorithm: filter based on archival data
  • Redistribute

docs.title: Methodology
docs.text:
Framework for constructing comparable city portraits through spatial, documentary, and data-based analysis.

The methodology translates heterogeneous sources into structured representations of urban systems, enabling reconstruction, comparison, and interpretation across contexts.

docs.title: Insights
docs.text:
Contextual Analysis

Extraction of historical, institutional, and narrative information from archives, publications, and actor networks. Outputs are structured descriptions of periods, decisions, and transformations.

Spatial Analysis

Derivation of urban structure from mapped data. Includes morphology, parcel logic, building distribution, and relationships between elements across scales.

Data Analysis

Quantification of attributes such as building types, densities, and distributions. Combines multiple datasets and resolves inconsistencies through alignment and calibration.

docs.text:
01 Collection

Acquisition of spatial data, written sources, and statistical datasets.

02 Alignment

Linking sources through shared categories such as time periods, typologies, and locations.

03 Derivation

Extraction of patterns such as typological distributions, spatial structures, and temporal shifts.

04 Synthesis

Construction of consistent representations of the city for comparison across regions and time.

  • Compare derived distributions with known references

docs.text: Application Area Use Cases Base Mapping Reference layer creation, coordinate system standardization, spatial reference establishment Environmental Assessment Biodiversity mapping, protected area analysis, environmental impact evaluation Infrastructure Analysis Utility network mapping, infrastructure asset management, service coverage analysis Socio-Economic Mapping Demographic analysis, economic development mapping, social service accessibility Geospatial Integration Data harmonization, coordinate system alignment, multi-source data integration

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: • Albanian Spatial Information Gateway

docs.title: Census
docs.text: National census data provides demographic, housing, and socio-economic statistics for population analysis and urban planning.

docs.title: Data Content
docs.text: Category Content Examples Population Statistics Demographic counts and distributions Age groups, gender ratios, population density by administrative unit Housing Data Residential building and occupancy information Housing types, occupancy rates, building conditions, household sizes Economic Indicators Employment and income data Labor force participation, occupation types, income levels, economic activity Education Levels Educational attainment and literacy School attendance, educational qualifications, literacy rates Migration Patterns Population movement and mobility Internal migration, urban-rural flows, international migration Household Characteristics Family structure and living conditions Family size, household composition, access to services

docs.title: Applications in CityPortraits
docs.text: Application Area Use Cases Demographic Analysis Population distribution mapping, age structure analysis, gender balance assessment Housing Market Analysis Housing density mapping, residential development patterns, housing quality assessment Socio-Economic Planning Economic activity mapping, employment center identification, income distribution analysis Service Planning Education facility planning, healthcare access analysis, infrastructure demand assessment Urban Growth Modeling Population growth projections, migration impact analysis, settlement pattern prediction Equity Analysis Social service accessibility, economic disparity mapping, community needs assessment

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: • National Institute of Statistics Albania

docs.title: GDE
docs.text: Global Dynamic Exposure project provides building-by-building exposure models for risk monitoring in natural catastrophes.

docs.title: Data Content
docs.text: Category Content Examples Building Footprints Individual building geometries Residential, commercial, and public buildings Building Properties Structural and occupancy attributes Building type, height, construction material, occupancy class Land Use Data CORINE land cover and urban classification Urban fabric, agricultural land, forest areas Administrative Divisions Qark, municipality, and commune boundaries Tirana municipality, Berat district, administrative units Infrastructure Networks Transportation and utility systems Road networks, power lines, water systems Geographic Features Natural and man-made features Rivers, elevation data, protected areas

docs.title: Exposure Indicators
docs.text: Indicator Type Data Sources Applications Building Exposure Footprints, properties, occupancy Earthquake risk assessment, flood vulnerability Vulnerability Assessment Construction type, age, resilience measures Structural vulnerability mapping, retrofit prioritization Urban Structure Land use, density, connectivity City morphology analysis, development pattern monitoring Dynamic Monitoring Temporal changes, urban growth Risk evolution tracking, climate adaptation planning

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text:
• GDE Data Explorer
  • Global Dynamic Exposure

docs.title: Integration
docs.text: GDE data provides the administrative framework for integrating OSM street data and GHSL settlement information.

docs.title: GHSL
docs.text: Global Human Settlement Layer provides global built-up area mapping and population density data for urban analysis.

docs.title: Data Content
docs.text: Category Content Examples Built-up Areas Urban and rural settlement mapping City centers, suburban areas, rural settlements Population Density Gridded population estimates High-density urban cores, medium-density suburbs Built-up Volume 3D building height and volume data High-rise districts, low-rise residential areas Settlement Classification Urban/rural typology Large urban areas, small towns, rural settlements Temporal Evolution Historical settlement changes Urban expansion patterns, growth trajectories

docs.title: Applications in CityPortraits
docs.text: Application Area Use Cases Urban Extent Mapping City boundary delineation, urban growth monitoring, sprawl analysis Population Distribution Demographic density mapping, urban population estimation, settlement patterns Built Environment Analysis Building density assessment, urban morphology studies, vertical development analysis Change Detection Historical urban transformation, development trend analysis, land use change monitoring

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text:
• GHSL Data Portal
  • European Commission JRC
  • Global Human Settlement Layer

docs.title: Integration
docs.text: GHSL data is combined with OSM for street network analysis and census data for demographic validation.

docs.title: OSM
docs.text: OpenStreetMap provides street networks, points of interest, and land use data for Albanian cities.

docs.title: Data Content
docs.text: Category Content Examples Street Networks Road infrastructure and transportation Highways, streets, bridges, pedestrian paths Points of Interest Public and commercial facilities Schools, hospitals, shops, government buildings Land Use Urban zoning and development types Residential areas, commercial zones, industrial sites Green Spaces Parks and recreational areas City parks, gardens, sports facilities Transportation Public transit and mobility Bus stops, railway stations, parking areas

docs.title: Applications in CityPortraits
docs.text: Application Area Use Cases Urban Morphology Street pattern analysis, urban grain identification, block structure assessment Accessibility Analysis Service coverage mapping, transportation network evaluation, pedestrian accessibility Economic Indicators Commercial density mapping, business concentration analysis, economic activity assessment Land Use Planning Mixed-use development evaluation, zoning analysis, urban growth monitoring

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text:
• OpenStreetMap Albania
  • Geofabrik Albania Extracts
  • OSMnx Library

docs.title: Integration
docs.text: OSM data is combined with GHSL for built-up area analysis and census data for demographic correlations.

docs.title: Spatial Analysis
docs.text: Spatial analysis examines the city as a layered system shaped by history, planning, and architecture.

docs.title: Urban Fabric
docs.text: Block structure, street networks, and spatial patterns. → Urban Fabric · Morphology · Emergent Patterns

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Building-level analysis of typology, construction, and classification. → Architecture · Typology · Construction · Taxonomy

docs.title: Approach
docs.text:
• Geospatial data (GIS, cadastral)
  • Historical sources
  • Computational methods
  • Architectural analysis

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: Reveal urban structure, link past and present, and support comparative city analysis.

docs.title: Model
docs.text: The model synthesizes spatial and data analysis into structured representations of urban systems.

docs.title: Spatial Timeline
docs.text: Map

docs.title: Graphs
docs.text:
Relational representation of spatial elements and their connections. → Graphs

Reset view Click a node to focus its neighborhood. Click the background or Reset view to return.

This is a sample network graph showing relationships between actors, events, and places. The actual graph will be rendered using the data from events.json, actors.json, and places.json.

docs.title: Patterns
docs.text:
Identification and abstraction of recurring spatial and architectural configurations. → Patterns

Morphological Analysis of urban form, street patterns, building density, and spatial structure. Architectural Study of building typologies, construction styles, and architectural evolution. Functional Examination of land use distribution, activity centers, and urban functionality.

docs.title: Approach
docs.text:
• Graph-based modeling
  • Pattern extraction
  • Rule-based systems
  • Computational representation

docs.title: Objective
docs.text: Translate urban complexity into structured models for analysis, comparison, and simulation.
